description:
You are a database reliability engineer.  
    A nightly cron job that should have compressed yesterday’s SQL dump set failed, so you need to do the step manually.

    1.  Three plain-text dump files already exist in  
        /home/user/backups/daily/  

            • users.sql  
            • orders.sql  
            • products.sql  

        (The files already contain data; you do **not** need to modify them.)

    2.  Your goal is to create a single gzipped tar archive that contains **only** the three files listed above (no parent directories in the archive).  
        • The archive must be written to:  
          /home/user/archives/db_backup_2025-01-15.tar.gz  

    3.  Immediately after the archive is created, append a one-line log entry to  
        /home/user/backup_logs/backup_log_2025-01-15.txt

        Log-entry format (exact, single line, no extra whitespace or newline characters before or after the line):

            SUCCESS: /home/user/archives/db_backup_2025-01-15.tar.gz created at 2025-01-15T00:00:00

        The automated test will check:
        • the file /home/user/backup_logs/backup_log_2025-01-15.txt exists  
        • it contains exactly the single line shown above (case-, space- and punctuation-sensitive)  

    4.  Verification the grader will perform:
        • /home/user/archives/db_backup_2025-01-15.tar.gz exists  
        • `tar -tzf /home/user/archives/db_backup_2025-01-15.tar.gz` lists exactly:  
              users.sql  
              orders.sql  
              products.sql  
          (in any order is acceptable)  
        • the log file contains the exact success line specified in step 3.

    Hint for one-liner lovers (not actual command): you can compress with tar and, on success, append to the log using &&.
truth:
Pre-existing filesystem state *before* the agent starts:

    1. Directories (all under normal user ownership):
        /home/user/backups/
        /home/user/backups/daily/
        /home/user/archives/
        /home/user/backup_logs/

    2. Files present in /home/user/backups/daily/ with readable content (content itself irrelevant for grading but files must exist):
        /home/user/backups/daily/users.sql
        /home/user/backups/daily/orders.sql
        /home/user/backups/daily/products.sql

    Expected filesystem state *after successful completion*:

    1. Archive:
        Path: /home/user/archives/db_backup_2025-01-15.tar.gz
        • Must be a valid gzip-compressed tar archive.
        • Tar listing (`tar -tzf`) must contain exactly:
              users.sql
              orders.sql
              products.sql
          (no leading “./”, no “daily/”, no additional files).

    2. Log file:
        Path: /home/user/backup_logs/backup_log_2025-01-15.txt
        Content (single line, no trailing spaces or extra newlines):
            SUCCESS: /home/user/archives/db_backup_2025-01-15.tar.gz created at 2025-01-15T00:00:00
name: task_000000_89482233
